faithfulness.justification: The tutor provides detailed formal statements of the Dirichlet principle, including a general form with ceiling function and an alternative formulation, none of which appear in the retrieved chunks. The chunks only mention the principle's name in a table of contents without any formal statement or mathematical details.
evidence: If \(n\) objects are placed into \(m\) boxes and \(n > m\), then at least one box contains at least two objects.
If \(N\) objects are distributed among \(k\) boxes, then some box contains at least \[ \left\lceil \frac{N}{k} \right\rceil \] objects.
if each box can contain at most \(r-1\) objects, then to place \(N\) objects into \(k\) boxes with \(N > k(r-1)\) is impossible
This principle is used mainly in **existence proofs** to show that certain configurations must exist.

evidence: Ground truth: 'if more than n objects are put into n boxes, then at least one box has at least 2 objects'
